title: Caregiver - Aadhaar-Based PwD Lookup - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the Aadhaar-Based PwD Lookup feature on the AT/AD platform. This feature allows caregivers to verify a PwD's Aadhaar number to determine if they already exist in SwarajAbility. Covers Aadhaar input validation (12-digit numeric, mandatory), Verify button enablement logic, SwarajAbility lookup with two outcomes (PwD found → OTP verification → auto-population, PwD not found → new registration), back navigation, and edge cases including invalid format, service unavailability, excessive attempts lock, and Aadhaar already linked to another caregiver.

inScope: Verify Aadhaar Number button enablement logic
PwD found flow: OTP sent to PwD mobile, OTP verification, auto-population
PwD not found flow: redirect to new registration
Back button navigation to adding new PwD
Aadhaar already linked to another caregiver block
outOfScope: Auto-population of PwD details (covered in SCRUM-369)
New PwD registration form (covered in SCRUM-372)

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar input field is mandatory and accepts only 12-digit numeric input
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; Navigated to Add New PwD / Aadhaar lookup page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Aadhaar-based PwD lookup page
2. Verify Aadhaar number input field is displayed
3. Verify the field is marked as mandatory
4. Leave the field empty and check if Verify button is disabled
5. Enter less than 12 digits: '12345678'
6. Verify Verify button remains disabled
7. Enter more than 12 digits: '1234567890123'
8. Verify field restricts to 12 digits or shows error
9. Enter alphabetic characters: 'abcdefghijkl'
10. Verify field rejects non-numeric input
11. Enter special characters: '!@#$%^&*()_+'
12. Verify field rejects special characters
13. Enter valid 12-digit Aadhaar: '234567890123'
14. Verify Verify Aadhaar Number button becomes enabled
expectedResults: Aadhaar field is displayed and marked as mandatory
Verify button is disabled when field is empty
Verify button is disabled for less than 12 digits
Field restricts input to 12 digits or shows validation error for excess
Non-numeric characters are rejected or trigger validation error
Special characters are rejected
Verify button is enabled only for valid 12-digit numeric input

testCases.title: Verify successful Aadhaar lookup when PwD is found in SwarajAbility
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; A PwD with the test Aadhaar exists in SwarajAbility
steps: 1. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page
2. Enter a valid 12-digit Aadhaar number of an existing PwD
3. Verify 'Verify Aadhaar Number' button is enabled
4. Click 'Verify Aadhaar Number' button
5. Verify system checks Aadhaar against SwarajAbility
6. Verify a clear message is shown indicating PwD was found
7. Verify OTP is sent to the PwD's registered mobile number
8. Verify OTP input field is displayed
9. Enter the correct OTP received
10. Click Verify/Submit OTP
11. Verify OTP verification succeeds
12. Verify the PwD is added to caregiver's care network (auto-population flow)
expectedResults: System successfully looks up Aadhaar in SwarajAbility
Clear message indicates PwD was found
OTP is sent to PwD's registered mobile
OTP input field appears after successful lookup
Correct OTP verifies successfully
Verified PwD is added to caregiver's network via auto-population flow
testCases.postconditions: PwD is found and verified via OTP; Auto-population flow initiated

testCases.title: Verify invalid OTP shows error message during PwD verification
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has entered valid Aadhaar; PwD found in SwarajAbility; OTP sent to PwD mobile
steps: 1. After PwD is found, verify OTP input field is displayed
2. Enter an incorrect OTP: '000000'
3. Click Verify/Submit OTP
4. Verify invalid OTP error message is displayed
5. Verify user can retry with correct OTP
6. Enter the correct OTP
7. Click Verify/Submit OTP
8. Verify successful verification after retry
expectedResults: Invalid OTP triggers clear error message (e.g., 'Invalid OTP. Please try again.')
User is allowed to retry OTP entry
Correct OTP after failed attempt still verifies successfully
PwD is added after successful OTP verification
testCases.postconditions: Invalid OTP handling is verified; Retry succeeds with correct OTP

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar lookup when PwD is NOT found redirects to new registration
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; The test Aadhaar does NOT exist in SwarajAbility
steps: 1. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page
2. Enter a valid 12-digit Aadhaar number not registered in SwarajAbility
3. Click 'Verify Aadhaar Number' button
4. Verify system checks Aadhaar against SwarajAbility
5. Verify a clear message is shown indicating PwD was NOT found
6. Verify user is redirected to new PwD registration flow
7. Verify the Aadhaar number is pre-filled in the registration form (if applicable)
8. Verify the registration form is ready for input
expectedResults: System checks Aadhaar and determines PwD is not found
Clear message indicates PwD not found in SwarajAbility
User is redirected to new PwD registration flow
Aadhaar may be pre-filled in registration form
Registration form is functional and ready for input
testCases.postconditions: PwD not found; User is on new registration flow

testCases.title: Verify 'Verify Aadhaar Number' button is enabled only for valid 12-digit input
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is on Aadhaar lookup page
steps: 1. Verify button is disabled when field is empty
2. Enter 1 digit, verify button is disabled
3. Enter 6 digits, verify button is disabled
4. Enter 11 digits, verify button is disabled
5. Enter 12 digits (valid), verify button becomes enabled
6. Delete one digit (back to 11), verify button becomes disabled again
7. Re-enter the 12th digit, verify button re-enables
expectedResults: Button is disabled for any input less than 12 digits
Button enables exactly at 12 valid numeric digits
Button dynamically toggles based on input length
No manual page refresh needed for button state change

testCases.title: Verify back button navigates to adding new PwD page
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is on Aadhaar lookup page
steps: 1. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page
2. Locate the back button
3. Click the back button before entering any data
4. Verify user is navigated back to adding new PwD / My PwDs page
5. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page again
6. Enter a partial Aadhaar number
7. Click the back button
8. Verify user is navigated back without errors
9. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page again
10. Verify the Aadhaar field is cleared (fresh state)
expectedResults: Back button is visible and clickable
Clicking back navigates to adding new PwD / My PwDs page
Back navigation works with empty or partial input
No errors occur during back navigation
Returning to lookup page shows fresh/cleared state

testCases.title: Verify invalid Aadhaar length/format shows validation error
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is on Aadhaar lookup page
steps: 1. Enter Aadhaar with less than 12 digits: '12345'
2. Attempt to click Verify (if enabled) or observe validation
3. Verify validation error message is displayed
4. Clear field and enter mixed alphanumeric: '12345abc9012'
5. Verify validation error or input rejection
6. Clear field and enter Aadhaar starting with 0: '012345678901'
7. Verify if leading zero is accepted or rejected
8. Clear field and enter all zeros: '000000000000'
9. Verify validation behavior for all-zero input
expectedResults: Short Aadhaar triggers validation error or button remains disabled
Alphanumeric input is rejected or triggers error
Validation error messages are clear and actionable
All-zero input may be rejected as invalid Aadhaar

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar already linked to another caregiver blocks access with guidance
testCases.preconditions: A PwD's Aadhaar is already linked to a different caregiver account
steps: 1. Log in as a different caregiver (not the one linked to the PwD)
2. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page
3. Enter the Aadhaar number already linked to another caregiver
4. Click 'Verify Aadhaar Number'
5. Verify access is blocked
6. Verify an error message indicates Aadhaar is already linked
7. Verify guidance is provided (e.g., 'Contact support' or help link)
8. Verify no PwD data is exposed to the unauthorized caregiver
expectedResults: Access is blocked for Aadhaar linked to another caregiver
Error message clearly indicates Aadhaar is already linked
Guidance is provided (support contact or help link)
No PwD data is exposed to the unauthorized caregiver
Caregiver remains on lookup page or is redirected appropriately

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar input field masks the entered number
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is on Aadhaar lookup page
steps: 1. Navigate to Aadhaar lookup page
2. Click on the Aadhaar input field
3. Enter a 12-digit Aadhaar number: '234567890123'
4. Verify the input is masked (e.g., XXXX-XXXX-0123 or dots)
5. Verify the full number is not visible in plain text
6. Verify the Verify button still works with masked input
expectedResults: Aadhaar input is masked for privacy (partial or full masking)
Full 12-digit number is not visible in plain text on screen
Verify button functions correctly with masked input
Masking does not interfere with validation logic

AC1_aadhaar_mandatory_12digit.description: Aadhaar number is mandatory, accepts only 12-digit numeric input

AC2_verify_button_enablement.description: Verify Aadhaar Number button enabled only for valid input

AC4_pwd_found_otp_flow.description: PwD found → OTP sent to PwD mobile, verified user added, invalid OTP message

AC5_pwd_not_found_registration.description: PwD not found → new registration flow

AC6_back_button.description: Caregiver can go back to adding new PwD by clicking back button

EC2_already_linked.description: Aadhaar already linked to another caregiver → access blocked with guidance

assumptions: Caregiver is registered and logged in (SCRUM-357, SCRUM-360)
My PwDs page is functional with available slots (SCRUM-363)
SwarajAbility Aadhaar lookup service is available in QA
OTP delivery to PwD mobile is functional in QA
Test Aadhaar numbers are available for both found and not-found scenarios
An Aadhaar linked to another caregiver is available for testing
dependencies: SCRUM-363: My PwDs page must be functional for navigation context
SCRUM-369: Auto-populate flow must be available for PwD found scenario
SCRUM-372: New PwD registration must be available for PwD not found scenario
SwarajAbility Aadhaar verification API must be operational
OTP delivery infrastructure must be functional

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is the Aadhaar input masked on the lookup page (similar to registration)?
Does the OTP for PwD verification have the same expiry/retry rules as registration OTPs?
What specific error message is shown when Aadhaar is linked to another caregiver?

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_AADHAAR_001-004) should be executed first - core input validation, found/not-found flows, OTP
High priority tests (005-008) cover button logic, back navigation, invalid format, already linked
Medium priority test (009) covers input masking

estimated_effort.critical_path: 4-6 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible
OTP delivery service functional for PwD mobile
Test Aadhaar numbers for found, not-found, and already-linked scenarios
Caregiver account with available PwD slots

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria covered; service unavailability and excessive attempts edge cases removed
